phases.name: WebSocket Origin Security
phases.description: Fix the security TODO at internal/audit/stream.go:80. The audit event WebSocket streamer currently returns true for all origin checks, allowing any origin to connect. Implement proper origin validation by parsing the Origin header from WebSocket handshake requests and validating against a configurable whitelist of allowed origins. Add configuration AUDIT_ALLOWED_ORIGINS to support comma-separated domain list. Default to localhost in development, require explicit configuration for production. This prevents cross-site WebSocket hijacking attacks.
success_criteria: Unit tests cover valid/invalid/missing origin scenarios

phases.name: Ed25519 Key Support
phases.description: Implement Ed25519 support in internal/oauth/keys.go:205 as a more modern alternative to RSA/ECDSA. Ed25519 offers faster signing, smaller signatures, and equivalent security at 128-bit level. Add Ed25519 as an option in the KeyType enum, implement GenerateEd25519KeyPair, SignEd25519, and VerifyEd25519 methods. Update the JWKS endpoint to expose Ed25519 public keys in correct format (crv: Ed25519). Add database migration to store Ed25519 key metadata. Include configuration option PREFERRED_KEY_TYPE to allow system-wide selection.
success_criteria: JWKS endpoint serves Ed25519 keys correctly

phases.name: Integration Test Coverage
phases.description: Expand integration test coverage beyond current 31 passing tests. The project has 349 Go files but only 100 test files indicating gaps. Add integration tests for critical cross-service flows: OAuth authorization code flow with PKCE, SCIM provisioning with webhook notifications, audit event streaming to WebSocket clients, policy evaluation via OPA integration, and multi-step governance review workflows. Use testcontainers for PostgreSQL, Redis, and Elasticsearch dependencies. Ensure tests can run in CI/CD pipeline.

phases.name: Frontend TSX Migration
phases.description: The diagnosis reports 0 TSX files despite having a React frontend. Investigate the frontend structure in web/admin-console and migrate JavaScript components to TypeScript. Add TypeScript strict mode configuration, create type definitions for API responses, and convert component files to .tsx. This will catch type errors at build time and improve developer experience. Radix UI components should be properly typed.
success_criteria: Frontend builds with TypeScript strict mode
API types defined in shared types file
No any types used without justification

phases.name: Kubernetes Production Hardening
phases.description: Production-ready Kubernetes manifests in deployments/kubernetes/helm. Add pod security policies, network policies restricting inter-service communication, resource quotas and limits for all services, healthcheck and liveness probes, PodDisruptionBudgets for high availability, and secrets management via external secrets operator. Configure horizontal pod autoscaling based on CPU/memory metrics. Add monitoring with ServiceMonitor for Prometheus integration.

phases.name: API Performance Optimization
phases.description: Profile and optimize API response times. Add database connection pooling optimization, implement Redis query result caching with proper invalidation, add database indexes for common query patterns, and implement pagination for all list endpoints. Use pprof to identify CPU and memory hotspots. Add response compression middleware. Implement optimistic locking for update operations to prevent race conditions.
success_criteria: Cache hit rate >80% for hot paths
Database query plans show index usage
API p95 latency <200ms for common operations

phases.name: Compliance Report Generation
phases.description: Complete the audit service compliance reporting capabilities. Implement reports for SOC 2, ISO 27001, and GDPR compliance. Add templates for each compliance framework, data extraction queries from Elasticsearch audit logs, PDF generation with charts showing access patterns, and scheduling for automated report generation. Include configurable retention policies and report signing for integrity verification.
success_criteria: Reports export to PDF with digital signature

phases.name: Observability and Alerting
phases.description: Implement comprehensive observability stack. Add structured logging correlation IDs across all services for request tracing. Integrate OpenTelemetry for distributed tracing. Create Grafana dashboards for key metrics: request rates, error rates, latency percentiles, authentication success/failure, and resource utilization. Configure Prometheus alerting rules for critical conditions. Add dead letter queue processing for failed async operations.
success_criteria: Correlation IDs propagate through all services
Dead letter queue monitoring in place

rationale: Project is in excellent health with passing builds and tests. The ordering prioritizes security first (WebSocket origin validation is a deployed vulnerability), then cryptographic enhancements (Ed25519), followed by test coverage expansion to ensure reliability. Frontend modernization comes next as the current 0 TSX count indicates a technical debt item. DevOps hardening follows to support production readiness. Performance optimization and additional features round out the roadmap once foundational stability is ensured.
